title: Natural-language processing deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap for Natural-language processing; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Natural-language processing: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? One English benchmark or text-overlap metric establishes that an NLP system understands language and works for all dialects.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Frame the exact language task and population, evaluate task-specific errors and coverage slices, and include qualified human review where metrics miss meaning or harm.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Frame the exact language task and population, evaluate task-specific errors and coverage slices, and include qualified human review where metrics miss meaning or harm.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Frame the exact language task and population, evaluate task-specific errors and coverage slices, and include qualified human review where metrics miss meaning or harm.
One English benchmark or text-overlap metric establishes that an NLP system understands language and works for all dialects.

answer.explanation: Frame the exact language task and population, evaluate task-specific errors and coverage slices, and include qualified human review where metrics miss meaning or harm.

items.title: Natural-language processing: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'coverage slices'. Frame the exact language task and population, evaluate task-specific errors and coverage slices, and include qualified human review where metrics miss meaning or harm.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Frame the exact language task and population, evaluate task-specific errors and coverage slices, and include qualified human review where metrics miss meaning or harm.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Frame the exact language task and population, evaluate task-specific errors and [[checkpoint]], and include qualified human review where metrics miss meaning or harm.

answer.explanation: Frame the exact language task and population, evaluate task-specific errors and coverage slices, and include qualified human review where metrics miss meaning or harm.
